name: Hypertension - Elevated Blood Pressure Vital Signs Query
description: Tests ability to find hypertension patients via elevated blood pressure measurements. The PheKB resistant-hypertension algorithm uses SBP > 140 mmHg or DBP > 90 mmHg on at least two outpatient measurements. This is the **first vital-signs-driven test case** in the suite — exercising LOINC codes for BP that are reported as Observation resources but at vital-signs not laboratory category. Tests whether LLM knows BP LOINC codes (8480-6 for SBP, 8462-4 for DBP) and the 140/90 threshold.
prompt: Find vital-signs observations for systolic blood pressure measurements where the value is greater than 140 mmHg, indicating hypertension.

prompts.naive: Find patients with high blood pressure readings.
prompts.broad: Find Observation resources for systolic blood pressure measurements where the value is greater than 140 mmHg. Sustained SBP > 140 (or DBP > 90) on multiple outpatient measurements meets standard hypertension diagnostic criteria.
prompts.expert: I need Observation resources for elevated systolic blood pressure. Use LOINC 8480-6 (Systolic blood pressure). Apply a value-quantity filter with the gt prefix at threshold 140, unit mm[Hg]. The query should look like: Observation?code=http://loinc.org|8480-6&value-quantity=gt140. Note that BP is also stored as a panel observation with components — this query targets the standalone systolic component code. Diastolic BP would use LOINC 8462-4 with threshold > 90.

metadata.algorithm_path_description: Patients with elevated SBP measurements (Paths A and D). The PheKB resistant-HTN algorithm requires SBP > 140 or DBP > 90 on at least two outpatient measurements. This test case exercises vital-signs Observation queries — distinct from lab Observation queries used by other phenotypes.
